summary:
The provided literature does not support the claim that Indigenous Australians experience premature biological aging; instead, observed poor health outcomes stem from socioeconomic disparities and environmental adversities. While epigenetic changes due to trauma might influence disease risk, there is no direct evidence for accelerated physiological aging processes. 

Note: This summary focuses only on clinically significant findings directly tied to the query regarding premature aging as a medical phenomenon. It excludes sociocultural context beyond its relevance to health determinants.

(End of Summary)

The available evidence shows no indication of biologically driven premature aging among Indigenous Australians. Observed adverse health trends result primarily from structural inequities such as poverty, access barriers, and intergenerational trauma affecting disease onset—not an early decline in biological function.  

(Note: Only core clinical facts linked explicitly to 'aging' have been included.)
